skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
stem: To study water content, samples from the stems of 30 plants were cut into cubes. One of these cubes has an edge length of 5.00 centimeters and a mass of 80.00 grams. What is the density of this cube, in grams per cubic centimeter?
rationale: The volume of the cube is (5.00 cm)3 = 125 cubic centimeters. Density equals mass divided by volume: 80.00 grams ÷ 125 cm³ = 0.64 grams per cubic centimeter. Note that 0.64 and 16/25 are equivalent answers.
